plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income and broad market exposure.
Increase QQQ to 20% to further enhance broad index exposure.
Trim SMH to 10% to reduce semiconductor sector concentration.
This plan aims for capital preservation with a significant cash buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative mode targets a modest ~1.5%/month, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but potential upside is also limited. Drawdowns should be minimal, but not immune to market downturns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop and to reduce leverage.
Trim SPAL to a small 2% to maintain some exposure while reducing overall leverage.
Trim QQQI to 15% due to its downtrend status and to rebalance the portfolio.
Buy ANET at 5% to add a high risk/reward quality tech setup.
Buy TXN at 5% to add another high risk/reward quality tech setup.
balanced.read: This balanced mode aims for ~2.5%/month growth with a risk-first approach. Leverage is significantly reduced, and concentration is managed, leading to a more stable portfolio. Expect moderate drawdowns, with a focus on quality growth and controlled risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop and to reallocate capital.
Trim QQQI to 10% due to its downtrend status and to free up capital for aggressive plays.
Buy NOW at 10% as it is the highest-scoring scanner setup.
Buy NVDU at 10% to add a high risk/reward tech setup.
Buy ANET at 10% to add another high risk/reward quality tech name.
aggressive.read: This aggressive mode targets ~4%/month growth by leaning into high-scoring setups and maintaining a higher risk profile. Expect significantly larger swings and potential drawdowns due to increased concentration and the inherent decay of leveraged ETFs (if used). Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage the amplified risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell SMH and CRWD to reduce high-beta and single-name exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income and covered-call exposure.
Increase QQQ to 20% to maintain some broad market exposure.
This plan aims to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
income.read: This income mode targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. While aiming for cash-like income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall during selloffs. This mode significantly reduces growth potential in favor of stability and yield.
